subject: Gaming
title: Dota 2: The International and Crowdfunding Prizes
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Dota 2: The International and Crowdfunding Prizes.

hook.text: The International's prize pool hit $40 million—funded by players buying in-game items.

buildup.text: Valve launched Dota 2 in 2013. Its complexity attracted hardcore players seeking the deepest MOBA.

discovery.text: The Battle Pass let fans contribute directly to the prize pool. Spending money meant supporting pros.

twist.text: Despite massive prizes, most Dota teams struggle financially. Only the top earners live comfortably.

climax.text: The International proved that fan funding could create the largest prize pools in esports history.

punchline.text: Fans funded a $40 million prize pool. Players built the prize themselves.

quiz.question: How is The International's prize pool funded?
options: Players buying in-game Battle Pass items contribute to it
